001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
026
027/**
028 * Serializes {@link SseEvent} objects to the <c>text/event-stream</c> wire format defined by the
029 * <a class="doclink" href="https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html">WHATWG Server-Sent Events spec</a>.
030 *
031 * <h5 class='topic'>Media types</h5>
032 * <p>
033 * Produces <c>Content-Type</c> types: <bc>text/event-stream</bc>.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
036 * <p>
037 * Accepts any of the following as input and emits one wire event per element followed by a blank
038 * line (the spec's "dispatch" boundary):
039 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
040 *    <li>A single {@link SseEvent}.
041 *    <li>{@link Iterable Iterable&lt;SseEvent&gt;}.
042 *    <li>An array {@code SseEvent[]}.
043 *    <li>{@link java.util.stream.Stream Stream&lt;SseEvent&gt;}.
044 * </ul>
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * After each event is written, the session calls {@link Writer#flush() Writer.flush()} so that downstream
048 * REST infrastructure flushes the underlying TCP socket and the event is delivered immediately to the
049 * client.
050 *
051 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
052 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
053 * </ul>
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
056 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Sse">SSE Basics</a>
057 * </ul>
058 */
059@SuppressWarnings({
060   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for serializer hierarchy
061   "java:S115", // Match AssertionUtils arg-name style used throughout Juneau.
062   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's serializer session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
063})
064public class SseSerializer extends WriterSerializer implements RecordWritable {
065
066   private static final String ARG_w = "w";
067
068   /**
069    * Wire-format media type produced by this serializer: <c>text/event-stream</c>.
070    */
071   public static final String MEDIA_TYPE = "text/event-stream";
072
073   /** Default serializer, all default settings. */
074   public static final SseSerializer DEFAULT = new SseSerializer(create());
075
076   /**
077    * Creates a new builder for this object.
078    *
079    * @return A new builder.
080    */
081   public static Builder create() {
082      return new Builder();
083   }
084
085   /**
086    * Builder class.
087    */
088   public static class Builder extends WriterSerializer.Builder<Builder> {
089
090      private static final Cache<HashKey,SseSerializer> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, SseSerializer.class).build();
091
092      /**
093       * Constructor, default settings.
094       */
095      protected Builder() {
096         produces(MEDIA_TYPE);
097         type(SseSerializer.class);
098      }
099
100      /**
101       * Copy constructor.
102       *
103       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
104       */
105      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
106         super(assertArgNotNull("copyFrom", copyFrom));
107      }
108
109      /**
110       * Copy constructor.
111       *
112       * @param copyFrom The serializer to copy from. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
113       */
114      protected Builder(SseSerializer copyFrom) {
115         super(assertArgNotNull("copyFrom", copyFrom));
116      }
117
118      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
119      public HashKey hashKey() {
120         return HashKey.of(super.hashKey());
121      }
122
123      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
124      public SseSerializer build() {
125         return cache(CACHE).build(SseSerializer.class);
126      }
127
128      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
129      public Builder copy() {
130         return new Builder(this);
131      }
132   }
133
134   /**
135    * Constructor.
136    *
137    * @param builder The builder for this object.
138    */
139   public SseSerializer(Builder builder) {
140      super(builder);
141   }
142
143   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
144   public Builder copy() {
145      return new Builder(this);
146   }
147
148   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
149   public SseSerializerSession.Builder createSession() {
150      return SseSerializerSession.create(this);
151   }
152
153   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
154   public SseSerializerSession getSession() {
155      return createSession().build();
156   }
157
158   /**
159    * Writes a comment line (heartbeat) to the supplied writer in the SSE wire format and flushes it.
160    *
161    * @param w The writer. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
162    * @param comment The comment text. {@code null} is treated as an empty string.
163    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.
164    */
165   @SuppressWarnings({
166      "java:S2095" // Writer ownership is the caller's; we only write/flush.
167   })
168   public static void writeComment(Writer w, String comment) throws IOException {
169      assertArgNotNull(ARG_w, w);
170      var c = comment == null ? "" : comment;
171      for (var line : c.split("\n", -1))
172         w.write(": " + line + "\n");
173      w.write("\n");
174      w.flush();
175   }
176
177   /**
178    * Convenience delegator that opens a {@link RecordWriter} over the output using
179    * <b>default session arguments</b> (mirrors {@link #serialize(Object)}).
180    *
181    * <p>
182    * The real implementation lives on {@link SseSerializerSession#serializeRecords(Object)}.
183    * Callers that need request-derived configuration should call {@link #createSession()} and
184    * invoke {@link SseSerializerSession#serializeRecords(Object)} on the built session instead.
185    *
186    * @param output The output.
187    * @return A new {@link RecordWriter}.
188    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
189    */
190   @Override /* RecordWritable */
191   public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
192      return getSession().serializeRecords(output);
193   }
194
195   @Override /* RecordWritable */
196   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
197      return false;
198   }
199}
